name: letak-worker-terno
description: Detects Terno leaflets under the current/future offer tabs and returns new leaflet URLs.
author: Fancysoft / 0ics

execute.description: Parse the Terno leaflet page (and each tab's Publitas viewer page) and return leaflets that are not already known.

outputPath.description: Absolute path where the full result JSON should be written.

knownLeaflets.description: Leaflet keys already seen, each formatted as 'type|from|to' (e.g. 'Leaflet|2026-06-29|2026-07-05'). Any leaflet whose key is not in this list is reported as new.

types.description: Optional case-insensitive leaflet-type filter. Terno only ever produces one leaflet type ('Leaflet'); this field exists for parity with the shared worker contract. When set, only leaflets of these types are returned; empty or absent means all types. Combined with knownLeaflets: a leaflet is returned only when it matches a type AND is new.

pageUrl.description: Override for the leaflet page URL. Defaults to https://terno.sk/sekcia/7-akciovy-letak

customData.description: Optional source-specific hints passed through from the scanner/orchestrator.

outputPath.description: Path to the written result JSON.

count.description: Number of returned leaflets (new AND matching the type filter).

hasNew.description: True when at least one matching new leaflet was found.

leaflets.description: Matched leaflets (new AND of a requested type), each a downloadable PDF entry.

type.description: Constant 'Leaflet' -- Terno only produces one leaflet format. Part of the novelty key. Deliberately NOT the current/future tab: the same leaflet moves from 'future' to 'current' as weeks pass, and the key must stay stable across that transition.

from.description: Approximate validity start date (YYYY-MM-DD; Monday of the ISO week embedded in the leaflet's Publitas slug -- Terno does not expose a real validity range, see CLAUDE.md).

to.description: Approximate validity end date (YYYY-MM-DD; Sunday of that same ISO week).

fileSize.description: Size of the result JSON in bytes.

terminate.description: Cancel a running scrape and clean up resources.
